questions.question: In which year was Jnanpith award instituted?
questions.explanation: Instituted in 1961, the award is bestowed only on Indian writers writing in Indian languages included in the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution of India and English. A cash prize of ₹12 lakh (equivalent to ₹47 lakh or US$73,000 in 2016), and a bronze replica of Saraswati, the Hindu goddess of knowledge and wisdom

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT an award presented by Sahitya Akademi?
questions.explanation: Sahitya Akademi annually presents the above mentioned awards except the Saraswathi Samman, which is presented by K.K.Birla Foundation for literary works. Along with the above mentioned awards, Sahitya Akademi also presents Prize for Translation award.

questions.question: "Festival of Letters" is organized which of the following organisations?
questions.explanation: Sahitya Akademi holds a week-long Festival of Letters in the month of February every year. It begins with the ceremony to present the Akademi's Annual Awards for creative writing. Awards are given away by the President of the Akademi.

questions.question: In which year was SAARC literary award constituted?
questions.explanation: SAARC Literary Award is an annual award conferred by the Foundation of SAARC Writers and Literature (FOSWAL) since 2001. It is conferred on writers from SAARC nations. FOSWAL also presents SAARC Young Writers Award. It also organizes variety of art, literature and culture related festivals ensuring participations of eminent writers and scholars from across South Asian region.

questions.question: The first award of Sangeet Natak Academy was presented in the year?
questions.explanation: The first award was presented in the year 1952. The Sangeet Natak Akademi was established by the then Ministry of Education, Government of India in 1952. The first award was given in the field of music.

questions.question: Bharat Ratna was established in which year?
questions.explanation: Bharat Ratna was first constituted in the year 1954. The award is conferred in recognition of exceptional service/performance of the highest order. It is the highest civilian award of India. Recipients receive a Sanad signed by the President and a peepal-leaf - shaped medallion.

questions.question: Which among the following organization/Individual has received Nobel Prize for maximum number of times?
options: International Committee of the Red Cross
questions.explanation: The International Committee of the Red Cross has received the Nobel Peace Prize three times, which is the highest so far. Six laureates have received more than one Nobel prize. The other above-mentioned organizations/individuals have received it twice.

questions.question: Who was the first recipient of Dadasaheb Phalke Award?
questions.explanation: Devika Rani was the first recipient of the award. She was awarded in the year 1969. She was honored at the 17th National Film Awards. She is regarded as the first lady of Indian cinema. She also founded the first Indian public limited film company, Bombay Talkies, in 1934.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian to receive Man Booker Prize?
questions.explanation: Arundathi Roy was the first Indian citizen to receive Man Booker Prize in the year 1997. Apart from being a novelist, she is also a political activist involved in human rights and environmental causes. She is also a recipient of Sydney Peace Prize.

questions.question: Which among the following institution/organization presents Oscar awards?
options: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences
U.S Department of Arts and Culture
questions.explanation: The Oscars are given annually by the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences. It includes totally 24 awards in different categories. The Oscars are also referred to as Academy Awards. The awards were first presented in 1929. It is given to recognize excellence in cinematic achievements.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a work of Jawaharlal Nehru?
options: Words of Freedom: Ideas of a Nation
questions.explanation: Annihilation of Caste was written by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar. It was against the country's concept of untouchability. It was an essay of undelivered speech of Ambedkar. The rest of were authored by Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.question: Who was the author of the book "Poverty and Un-British Rule in India"?
questions.explanation: The book was written by Dadabhai Naoroji. It talks about drain of India's wealth into Britain. It was one of the first works to talk about economic imperialism.

questions.question: Who among the following is the author of Neel Darpan?
questions.explanation: It is a Bengali Drama written by Dinbandhu Mitra. It deals with the hardships of Indigo farmers. It was also essential to the development of theatre in Bengal and it was the first drama staged in National Theatre of Calcutta.

questions.question: Which among the following is a work of Aurobindo Gosh?
questions.explanation: Savitri is a work of Sri Aurobindo Gosh. It is based on fundamentals of Mahabharata. It is regarded as one of the longest poem in English language. It consists of 12 books and was published in 2 parts.

questions.question: Who among the following is the author of "The White Tiger"?
questions.explanation: The White Tiger is the debut novel by Indian author Aravind Adiga. It won the Man Booker Prize. The work deals about India's class struggle in a globalized world.

questions.question: Which among the following literary work is the first to win Jnanpith Award?
questions.explanation: Odakkuzhal was the first literary work to receive Jnanpith Award in the year 1965. It was the work of Sankara Gurup and was in the language Malayalam.

questions.question: Who is the author of "Matoshree"?
questions.explanation: It was authored by Lok Sabha speaker Sumitra Mahajan. It is based on the life of Maratha Queen Ahilyabai Holkar.

questions.question: Who was the author of "Narendra Modi: A Political Biography"?
questions.explanation: Narendra Modi: A Political Biography was written by Andy Marino. It is a compilation on life of Prime Minister Narendra Modi based on the interview session of Andy Marino.

questions.question: "One life is not enough" is an autobiography of who among the following?
questions.explanation: One Life Is Not Enough is an autobiography by K. Natwar Singh. He served as an Minister of External Affairs in UPA government. The book talks about many political issues during his career.

questions.question: "True Colours" is written by who among the following?
questions.explanation: True Colours is an autobiography of Adam Gilchrist. It was published in 2008.

questions.question: Which of the following animal is NOT present in Saranath Pillar of Ashoka?
questions.explanation: Capital of Saranath Pillar has Lion at the top and on the abacus it has a galloping horse, a bull, a lion and an elephant. The capital of this pillar without lotus has been adopted as National emblem of India. Ashoka built the Sarnath pillar to commemorate the site of the first preaching of Lord Buddha, where he taught the Dharma to five monks- his first sermon after attaining enlightenment.

questions.question: Gombeyatta puppet theater belongs to which state?
questions.explanation: The string puppets of Karnataka are called Gombeyatta. They are styled and designed like the characters of Yakshagana, the traditional theatre form of the region. Episodes enacted in Gombeyatta are usually based on Prasangas of the Yakshagana plays. The music that accompanies is dramatic and beautifully blends folk and classical elements.

questions.question: Which among the following is NOT a classical dance form of India?
questions.explanation: Chhau is one of the most renowned tribal martial dances of Indian states of West Bangal, Jharkhand and Odisha. Based on the principles of laid down in Natyasastra there are 8 classical dances in India- Bharatanatyam, Kathakali, Kathak, Kuchipudi, Odissi, Sattriya, Manipuri, Mohiniyattam.

questions.question: Who is the author of the Natyashastra?
questions.explanation: Natyashastra is the work of Bharat Muni. It deals with art of drama, dance and music. It is generally accepted that the date of the work is between the 2nd century B.C.E- 2nd century C.E. The Natyashastra is also known as the fifth veda and referred to as Natyaveda. The text consists of 36 chapters with a cumulative total of 6000 poetic verses describing performance arts.

questions.question: Hemis Buddhist Monastery is situated in which state?
questions.explanation: Hemis Monastry is the Ladakh region of Jammu & Kashmir. It was built by King Singge Namgyal. Hemis Tsechuis a day long festival celebrated in the monastery. It is also famous for the sacred masked dance. The Hemis Festival is dedicated to Lord Padmasambhava.

questions.question: Art of arabesque was introduced by which of the following dynasty?
questions.explanation: Art of arabesque was introduced by Sultanate dynasty. In this art form, Arabic letters and verses of Quran were inscribed in the buildings as a form of decoration. Since Muslims forbid to replicate living forms on any surface, developed their religious art and architecture consisting of the arts of arabesque.

questions.question: Hawa Mahal is in which state of India?
questions.explanation: Hawa Mahal is in Jaipur of Rajasthan. It is a combination of Hindu and the Islamic traditions. The building is suited to the hot, dry climate of Rajasthan, by making the entire facade a perforated screen by creating over fifty slightly raised pavillions, each a half oriel window, to allow breeze pass through those hundreds of perforated jali windows. So it is also called as "Palace of the Wind".

questions.question: T.H.Vinayakram is related to which of the following?
questions.explanation: Padma Bhushan awardee T.H.Vinayakram is a Carnatic ghatam player. He has also received Grammy Award. The ghatam is one of the most ancient percussion instruments of South India. It is usually used in both Carnatic and folk music.

questions.question: Rukmini Devi Arundale is associated with which of the following?
questions.explanation: Rukmini Devi Arundale is a famous Bharatanatyam dancer and was founder of Kalakshetra. She is also known for her efforts towards the protection of animal rights and their welfare. She was awarded the Padma Bhushan and also received the "Sangeet Natak Akademi Fellowship". She was instrumental in transforming Bharatanatyam from its vulgar to classical, elegant form.

questions.question: Unakoti pilgrimage is related to which of the following sects?
questions.explanation: Unakoti is a Shaivite pilgrimage site in the state of Tripura. The site has huge rock reliefs celebrating Shiva. Ashokastami Mela is held in the month of April which is visited by thousands of pilgrims. Archaeological Survey of India has adopted it as a heritage site and has been recommended to UNESCO to be added as world heritage site.
